title: Error parsing chmod +x in ADD/COPY instructions
body:
### Issue Description

ADD/COPY instruction with `--chmod=+x` argument don't want to parse (while `--chmod=711` argument works)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Use ADD/COPY instruction with `--chmod=+x` argument in your Dockerfile
2. Build Dockerfile
3. Get Error parsing chmod +x


### Describe the results you received

```shell
Error: building at STEP "ADD --chmod=+x https://example.com /usr/local/bin": Error parsing chmod +x
Error: requires at least 1 arg(s), only received 0
```

### Describe the results you expected

It parses and then builds image

https://docs.docker.com/reference/dockerfile#copy---chmod

### Additional information

comments.body: The [Containerfile doc](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=latest#tag/containers/operation/ContainerCreateLibpod) doesn't mention the support for the symbolic notation, so it's more a `kind/feature` than a bug.

This seems like buildah is missing compatibility with Docker. 

WDYT? @Luap99 @nalind

Seems like an easy enough thing to add, which would increase compatibility with Dockerfiles originally written for Docker, so from my (still very new to the project) perspective it seems reasonable. This is the same as https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6066 , which I'd like to work on as a way to learn my way around more of the podman related codebase, if others agree it's worth doing.

> This seems like buildah is missing compatibility with Docker.

Obeying a string argument to `--chmod` instead of a number isn't a feature we have yet.  Doing it properly can get a bit complicated if you want to handle everything chmod(1) would accept.

> Obeying a string argument to `--chmod` instead of a number isn't a feature we have yet. Doing it properly can get a bit complicated if you want to handle everything chmod(1) would accept.

Handling everything docker handles shouldn't be too complicated, as we could use [this package](https://github.com/tonistiigi/dchapes-mode), which it appears docker are using too.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
